docs.title: Herald
docs.text:
Advanced Discord Bot for Hunter: The Reckoning 5th Edition

Herald is a comprehensive character management and dice rolling bot designed specifically for Hunter: The Reckoning 5th Edition gameplay. Beyond basic dice rolling, Herald provides advanced character tracking, H5E-specific mechanics, and quality-of-life tools to streamline your supernatural hunting experience.

docs.title: 🎭 Character Management
docs.text:
• Create and manage multiple Hunter characters
  • Full attribute and skill tracking
  • Automatic derived stat calculation
  • Character sheet display with H5E formatting

docs.title: 🎲 Advanced Dice Rolling
docs.text:
• H5E dice mechanics with successes and criticals
  • Character-integrated rolls (roll using traits)
  • Edge dice system
  • Desperation mechanics
  • Difficulty and danger levels
  • Clean, readable roll results

docs.title: ⚡ Hunter Mechanics
docs.text:
• Edge rating management
  • Desperation tracking
  • Creed, Ambition, Desire, and Drive
  • Health and Willpower damage tracking
  • Automatic messy critical handling

docs.title: 📈 Character Progression
docs.text:
• Experience point tracking and spending
  • Skill improvement system
  • XP transaction logs
  • Skill templates (Balanced, Specialist, Jack-of-all-trades)
  • Specialty management

docs.title: 🎒 Quality of Life
docs.text:
• Equipment and gear tracking
  • Character notes and journals
  • Bulk skill management
  • In-Discord help system
  • Autocomplete for commands

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start
docs.text:
Ready to start hunting? Here's how to get started with Herald:

  1. Add Herald to Your Server - Invite Herald and set permissions
  2. Create Your First Character - Make a Hunter in seconds
  3. Roll Some Dice - Learn the basics of H5E dice mechanics
  4. Explore Features - Discover everything Herald can do

docs.title: 📚 Documentation Sections
docs.text:
• Getting Started

    New to Herald? Start here for quick setup guides and basic tutorials.

    Get Started

  • Guides

    In-depth guides covering character creation, dice rolling, and H5E mechanics.

    Read Guides

  • Command Reference

    Complete reference for all Herald commands with examples.

    Browse Commands

  • H5E Integration

    Learn how Herald implements Hunter: The Reckoning 5E rules.

    Explore H5E

docs.title: 💬 Support & Community
docs.text:
Need help? Want to chat with other Hunters?

  • Join our Discord Server - Get support, report bugs, and suggest features
  • Check the FAQ - Answers to common questions
  • Report Issues - Found a bug? Let us know!

docs.title: 🏹 Why Herald?
docs.text:
Herald was built from the ground up for Hunter: The Reckoning 5th Edition. Unlike generic dice bots, Herald understands H5E mechanics:

  • H5E-Native: Designed specifically for Hunter, not adapted from other systems
  • Character Integration: Roll using your character's actual stats
  • Edge & Desperation: Full implementation of H5E's unique mechanics
  • Beginner Friendly: Clear commands, helpful errors, and in-Discord help
  • Always Improving: Regular updates based on community feedback

docs.title: 🎯 Example: Quick Roll
docs.text:
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Firearms difficulty:2

Herald automatically: - Looks up your character "Raven" - Gets their Dexterity and Firearms ratings - Rolls the combined pool - Checks for successes vs. difficulty 2 - Displays results with Edge/Desperation if applicable

docs.title: 📖 About Hunter: The Reckoning 5E
docs.text:
Hunter: The Reckoning 5th Edition is a tabletop RPG where you play as ordinary people who have been awakened to the supernatural world. Armed with determination, skills, and the mysterious power of the Edge, Hunters stand against monsters in the shadows.

Herald makes it easy to focus on the story while handling the mechanics behind the scenes.

Ready to hunt? → Get Started Now

docs.title: Command Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for all Herald commands organized by category.

docs.title: Command Categories
docs.text: Herald's commands are organized into logical categories based on their function:

docs.title: 🎭 Character Commands
docs.text:
Create, manage, and view your Hunter characters.

  • /create - Create a new character
  • /delete - Delete a character
  • /rename - Rename a character
  • /characters - List all your characters
  • /sheet - View character sheet

docs.title: 🎲 Dice Commands
docs.text:
Roll dice using H5E mechanics with or without characters.

  • /roll - Manual dice roll
  • /roll_char - Character-based roll
  • /simple - Simple dice pool
  • /rouse - Desperation rouse check

docs.title: 📈 Development Commands
docs.text:
Improve your character through skills, specialties, and experience.

  • /skill_set - Set individual skill dots
  • /skill_template - Apply skill templates
  • /skill_bulk - Set multiple skills at once
  • /specialty - Manage skill specialties
  • /specialty_bulk - Add multiple specialties
  • /xp - Manage experience points
  • /spend_xp - Spend XP for improvements
  • /xp_log - View XP transaction history

docs.title: ⚡ Hunter Mechanics
docs.text:
Manage H5E-specific Hunter traits and abilities.

  • /edge - Set Edge rating
  • /desperation - Track Desperation level
  • /creed - Set Hunter's Creed
  • /ambition - Set long-term Ambition
  • /desire - Set short-term Desire
  • /drive - Set hunting Drive
  • /damage - Apply health/willpower damage
  • /heal - Heal damage

docs.title: 🛠️ Utility Commands
docs.text:
Additional tools and character extras.

  • /equipment - Manage gear and items
  • /notes - Character journal and notes
  • /help - In-Discord help system

docs.title: Quick Command List
docs.text: Command Description Example /create Create new character /create name:Raven /sheet View character sheet /sheet character:Raven /roll_char Roll with character stats /roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Stealth /roll Manual dice roll /roll attribute:3 skill:2 difficulty:2 /skill_set Set a skill rating /skill_set character:Raven skill:Firearms dots:3 /specialty Add skill specialty /specialty character:Raven skill:Firearms specialty:Pistols /edge Set Edge rating /edge character:Raven edge:3 /desperation Set Desperation /desperation character:Raven desperation:2 /xp Award experience /xp character:Raven amount:5 reason:Session 12 /help Get help /help topic:Dice Rolling

docs.title: Command Syntax Conventions
docs.text: Herald uses Discord's slash command system. Here's how to read command syntax:

docs.title: Required Parameters
docs.text:
Parameters without brackets are required:

/create name:Raven

You must provide a name.

docs.title: Optional Parameters
docs.text:
Parameters in [square brackets] are optional:

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity [skill:Stealth]

You can include or omit the skill parameter.

docs.title: Choices
docs.text:
Some parameters have predefined choices shown in a dropdown:

/roll_char attribute:Dexterity  ← Shows dropdown of all 9 attributes
/skill_template template:Balanced  ← Shows: Balanced, Specialist, Jack-of-all-trades

docs.title: Autocomplete
docs.text:
Character names and skills use autocomplete - start typing and Herald suggests options:

/sheet character:Ra  ← Shows "Raven", "Rachel", etc.

docs.title: Command Response Types
docs.text: Herald responds to commands in two ways:

docs.title: Public Responses
docs.text:
Results appear in the channel for everyone to see:

  • Dice rolls (by default)
  • Character sheets (optional)
  • XP awards

docs.title: Ephemeral Responses (You Only)
docs.text:
Some responses only you can see:

  • Error messages
  • Character lists
  • Help menus
  • Private character sheets (if requested)

Privacy

Use the ephemeral:True parameter on commands like /sheet to keep results private!

docs.title: Rolling Dice
docs.text:
# Manual roll
/roll attribute:3 skill:2 difficulty:2

# With character
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Firearms difficulty:2

# With Edge
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Wits skill:Awareness edge:3

# With Desperation
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Strength skill:Brawl desperation:True

docs.title: Managing Characters
docs.text:
# Create
/create name:Raven strength:2 dexterity:4 intelligence:3

# View
/sheet character:Raven

# Update attributes (through XP or character creation)
/create name:Raven strength:3  # Updates existing character

# Delete
/delete character:Raven

docs.title: Setting Skills
docs.text:
# One at a time
/skill_set character:Raven skill:Firearms dots:3

# Quick template
/skill_template character:Raven template:Balanced

# Multiple skills
/skill_bulk character:Raven skill1:Firearms dots1:3 skill2:Stealth dots2:4

docs.title: Need More Details?
docs.text:
Each command category page provides:

  • Detailed parameter descriptions
  • Usage examples
  • Tips and best practices
  • Common mistakes to avoid
  • Related commands

Browse the command categories above or use /help in Discord for interactive guidance!

docs.title: Adding Herald to Your Server
docs.text: Learn how to invite Herald to your Discord server and configure permissions.

docs.title: Step 1: Invite Herald
docs.text:
Click the invite link below to add Herald to your server:

Invite Herald to Your Server

docs.title: Step 2: Select Your Server
docs.text:
1. A Discord authorization page will open
  2. Select the server where you want to add Herald from the dropdown
  3. Click Continue

Permissions Required

You must have the Manage Server permission to add bots to a Discord server.

docs.title: Step 3: Review Permissions
docs.text: Herald requests the following permissions:

docs.title: Required Permissions
docs.text:
• Send Messages - Respond to commands and send results
  • Embed Links - Display character sheets and dice results in formatted embeds
  • Use Slash Commands - Access to Discord's slash command system (automatic)

docs.title: Recommended Permissions
docs.text:
• Use External Emoji - Display custom dice emojis for better-looking rolls
  • Without this, Herald uses text-based dice results instead

docs.title: Optional Permissions
docs.text: None! Herald doesn't need any elevated permissions like managing roles, channels, or messages.

docs.title: Step 4: Authorize
docs.text:
Click Authorize to complete the installation.

Herald will join your server and be ready to use immediately!

docs.title: Check if Herald is Online
docs.text:
1. Look for Herald in your server's member list
  2. Herald should show as online (green status)
  3. If offline, Herald may be restarting or experiencing issues

docs.title: Test a Command
docs.text:
Try a simple command to verify Herald is working:

/help

If Herald responds with the help menu, everything is working correctly!

docs.title: Herald Isn't Responding
docs.text:
Check slash command permissions:

  1. Go to Server Settings → Integrations → Herald
  2. Ensure slash commands are enabled
  3. Check that the @everyone role (or your role) has Use Application Commands permission

Check channel permissions:

  1. Herald needs permission to view and send messages in channels where commands are used
  2. Right-click the channel → Edit Channel → Permissions
  3. Ensure Herald's role has Send Messages and View Channel enabled

docs.title: Dice Emojis Not Showing
docs.text:
Herald uses custom emojis to display dice results. If you see text instead of emoji:

Option 1: Enable External Emoji Permission 1. Give Herald the Use External Emoji permission

Option 2: Use Text Mode - Herald automatically falls back to text-based results - No configuration needed!

docs.title: Commands Not Appearing
docs.text:
If slash commands don't appear when you type /:

  1. Wait a few minutes - Discord can take time to sync commands
  2. Try restarting your Discord client
  3. Check that Herald has the Use Application Commands permission
  4. Try using the command anyway by typing it fully: /help

docs.title: Configuration Options
docs.text: Herald works out of the box with sensible defaults. However, you may want to:

docs.title: Set Up a Commands Channel
docs.text:
Create a dedicated channel for character management and dice rolling:

  1. Create a channel like #herald-rolls or #character-sheets
  2. Pin a message with common commands or the bot invite link
  3. Herald works in any channel where it has permissions

docs.title: Set Up Roles
docs.text:
Consider creating roles for:

  • Storytellers - Game masters running chronicles
  • Players - Active players in your games
  • Spectators - People who can view but not roll

Herald doesn't require special roles, but they can help organize your server!

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
✅ Herald is now installed and ready to use!

Continue to:

  • Create Your First Character - Make a Hunter
  • Quick Start Guide - Learn the basics
  • Command Reference - See all available commands

Still Having Issues?

Join our Support Server for help from the community and developers!

docs.title: Basic Dice Rolling
docs.text: Learn how to roll dice with Herald using H5E mechanics.

docs.title: Rolling Methods
docs.text: Herald offers three ways to roll dice:

docs.title: 1. Manual Roll (/roll)
docs.text:
Roll dice manually without a character:

/roll attribute:3 skill:2 difficulty:2

Best for: - Quick rolls - Rolling for NPCs - Testing dice mechanics - When you don't have a character

docs.title: 2. Character Roll (/roll_char)
docs.text:
Roll using your character's stats:

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Firearms difficulty:2

Best for: - Regular play - Character actions - When you want stat tracking - Automatic Edge/Desperation

docs.title: 3. Simple Roll (/simple)
docs.text:
Basic dice pool without H5E mechanics:

/simple pool:5

Best for: - Non-H5E rolling - Pure dice pools - Quick tests

docs.title: Dice Pool
docs.text:
Your dice pool = Attribute + Skill + Modifiers + Edge

Dexterity 4 + Firearms 3 + Edge 2 = 9 dice

docs.title: Successes
docs.text:
• 6-9 = Success (1 success)
  • 10 = Critical (2 successes)
  • 1-5 = Failure (no success)

docs.title: Difficulty
docs.text:
Difficulty is the target number of successes you need:

Difficulty Description 0 Automatic (no roll needed) 1 Simple task 2 Standard challenge 3 Hard task 4 Extreme challenge 5 Nearly impossible 6+ Legendary feat

docs.title: Margin
docs.text:
Margin = Total Successes - Difficulty

  • Margin 0+ = Success
  • Margin below 0 = Failure
  • Higher margin = Better success

docs.title: Simple Attack Roll
docs.text:
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Firearms difficulty:2

Herald shows: - Pool calculation (Dexterity 4 + Firearms 3 = 7 dice) - Dice results with emojis - Total successes - Whether you met the difficulty - Margin of success

docs.title: Investigation Check
docs.text: /roll_char character:Raven attribute:Wits skill:Investigation difficulty:3

docs.title: Stealth Action
docs.text: /roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Stealth difficulty:2

docs.title: Edge Dice
docs.text: Edge adds dice to your pool:

docs.title: Automatic Edge
docs.text:
Herald automatically adds your character's Edge rating:

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Strength skill:Brawl

# Herald adds Raven's Edge (2) automatically
# Pool = Strength 2 + Brawl 2 + Edge 2 = 6 dice

docs.title: Override Edge
docs.text:
Override your character's Edge for this roll:

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Strength skill:Brawl edge:4

# Uses Edge 4 instead of Raven's normal Edge 2

docs.title: No Edge
docs.text:
Roll without Edge:

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Strength skill:Brawl edge:0

docs.title: Desperation Dice
docs.text: Desperation dice are special - they can cause messy criticals!

docs.title: When Desperation Triggers
docs.text:
Herald uses your character's Desperation rating to determine when to roll Desperation dice:

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Wits skill:Awareness desperation:True

docs.title: Messy Criticals
docs.text:
If Desperation dice roll 10s:

  • You get the successes
  • BUT something goes wrong
  • Storyteller determines the complication

Messy Critical

When you see "Messy Critical!" in your roll result, you succeeded but at a cost. The Storyteller will describe what went wrong.

docs.title: Modifiers
docs.text: Add or subtract dice from your pool:

docs.title: Add Dice
docs.text:
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Stealth modifier:2

Good for: - Favorable circumstances - Equipment bonuses - Help from allies - Using specialties (Herald does this automatically!)

docs.title: Subtract Dice
docs.text:
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Strength skill:Athletics modifier:-2

Good for: - Penalties - Poor conditions - Injuries - Distractions

docs.title: Specialties
docs.text: When your specialty applies, Herald automatically adds +1 die!

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Character has: Firearms (Pistols)

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Firearms

# If using a pistol, Herald adds +1 automatically
# Storyteller confirms if specialty applies

Specialty Bonus

Talk to your Storyteller about when specialties apply. Herald tracks them but relies on you to use the right skill!

docs.title: Reading Roll Results
docs.text: Herald displays rolls in Inconnu style:

docs.title: Result Display
docs.text:
═══════════════════════════════
Raven
═══════════════════════════════

3 successes

Dexterity + Firearms = 7 dice

🎲 10 9 6 5 4 3 2

Margin: +1 (met difficulty 2)
═══════════════════════════════

What this means:

  • Character name - Who rolled
  • Successes - How many you got (10=crit, 6-9=success)
  • Pool - What you rolled
  • Dice - Individual results
  • Margin - How much you beat the difficulty by

docs.title: Success Types
docs.text: Result Description Critical! Double 10s - exceptional success X successes Standard success Failure Didn't meet difficulty Messy Critical! Success from Desperation 10s - with complication

docs.title: Contested Rolls
docs.text:
Both parties roll, highest successes wins:

# Player rolls
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Stealth

# Storyteller rolls for NPC
/roll attribute:3 skill:2 difficulty:0

# Compare totals

docs.title: Extended Actions
docs.text:
Roll multiple times, accumulating successes:

# Session 1
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Intelligence skill:Investigation
# 2 successes

# Session 2  
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Intelligence skill:Investigation
# 3 successes (total: 5)

# Keep tracking until you reach goal

docs.title: Teamwork
docs.text:
Multiple characters help:

# Helper rolls first
/roll_char character:Sarah attribute:Intelligence skill:Occult

# Primary actor gets bonus
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Intelligence skill:Occult modifier:2

docs.title: Combat
docs.text:
# Attack
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Firearms difficulty:2

# Defense
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Athletics difficulty:2

# Damage (if hit)
# Usually fixed by weapon, not rolled

docs.title: Social
docs.text:
# Persuasion
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Manipulation skill:Persuasion difficulty:2

# Intimidation
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Strength skill:Intimidation difficulty:3

# Insight
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Wits skill:Empathy difficulty:2

docs.title: Investigation
docs.text:
# Research
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Intelligence skill:Academics difficulty:3

# Search
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Wits skill:Investigation difficulty:2

# Track
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Wits skill:Survival difficulty:3

docs.title: 1. Describe Your Action
docs.text:
Tell the story before rolling:

"I peek around the corner, trying to spot the vampire without being seen."

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Wits skill:Awareness difficulty:2

docs.title: 2. Ask About Difficulty
docs.text:
Check with your Storyteller:

"How hard is this to pick this lock? Difficulty 2 or 3?"

docs.title: 3. Consider Edge Usage
docs.text:
Sometimes saving Edge for later is smart:

# Save Edge for important rolls
/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Stealth edge:0

docs.title: 4. Track Your Desperation
docs.text:
Know when Desperation might trigger:

"I'm at Desperation 3, so this roll might be messy..."

docs.title: Practice Rolls
docs.text:
Try these to get comfortable:

# Easy investigation (Difficulty 1)
/roll_char character:YourName attribute:Intelligence skill:Investigation difficulty:1

# Standard combat (Difficulty 2)
/roll_char character:YourName attribute:Dexterity skill:Firearms difficulty:2

# Hard challenge (Difficulty 3)
/roll_char character:YourName attribute:Strength skill:Athletics difficulty:3

# With modifiers
/roll_char character:YourName attribute:Wits skill:Awareness modifier:2 difficulty:2

Questions?

  • Use /help topic:Dice Rolling in Discord

docs.title: Creating Your First Character
docs.text: Let's create your first Hunter character step-by-step!

docs.title: The Quick Way
docs.text:
Want to jump right in? Use this single command:

/create name:Raven

Done! Herald creates a basic character with: - All attributes set to 1 - All skills set to 0 - Default Health (3) and Willpower (3) - No Edge or Desperation

You can customize everything later!

docs.title: The Detailed Way
docs.text:
Want more control from the start? Include attributes in your creation:

/create name:Raven strength:2 dexterity:4 stamina:3 charisma:3 manipulation:2 composure:3 intelligence:3 wits:3 resolve:2

Attribute Distribution

In H5E, new Hunters typically have:

  • Total of 12 dots across all 9 attributes
  • No attribute higher than 5
  • Minimum of 1 in each attribute (Herald's default)

docs.title: 1. Choose a Name
docs.text:
Pick a name for your Hunter. This is what you'll use to reference them in commands:

/create name:Raven

Character Names

  • Use quotes for multi-word names: name:"Sarah Connor"
  • Names are case-insensitive for commands
  • Each character name must be unique to you

docs.title: 2. Set Attributes
docs.text: Herald uses the 9 core H5E attributes:

docs.title: Physical
docs.text:
• Strength - Raw physical power
  • Dexterity - Agility and coordination
  • Stamina - Endurance and toughness

docs.title: Social
docs.text:
• Charisma - Charm and force of personality
  • Manipulation - Subtlety and persuasion
  • Composure - Self-control and poise

docs.title: Mental
docs.text:
• Intelligence - Reasoning and memory
  • Wits - Quick thinking and awareness
  • Resolve - Determination and focus

Example: 

/create name:Raven strength:2 dexterity:4 stamina:3 charisma:3 manipulation:2 composure:3 intelligence:3 wits:3 resolve:2

docs.title: 3. View Your Character
docs.text:
Check out your new character:

/sheet character:Raven

Herald displays your complete character sheet with: - All attributes - Derived stats (Health, Willpower) - Empty skills (ready to fill in) - Hunter traits (Creed, Edge, etc.)

docs.title: Setting Up Skills
docs.text: Now let's add skills to your character!

docs.title: Option 1: Skill Template (Fastest)
docs.text:
Apply a pre-built skill distribution:

/skill_template character:Raven template:Balanced

Available Templates:

  • Balanced (7/5/3/1) - Well-rounded Hunter
  • 1 skill at 4 dots
  • 3 skills at 3 dots
  • 4 skills at 2 dots

  • 4 skills at 1 dot

  • Specialist (9/5/1/0) - Focused expert

  • 1 skill at 5 dots
  • 2 skills at 4 dots
  • 3 skills at 3 dots

  • 3 skills at 2 dots

  • Jack-of-all-trades (5/5/5/0) - Broad competence

  • 8 skills at 2 dots
  • 4 skills at 1 dot

Skill Points

H5E characters typically start with 11 skill dots in their primary category, 7 in secondary, and 4 in tertiary.

docs.title: Option 2: Individual Skills
docs.text:
Set skills one at a time for complete control:

/skill_set character:Raven skill:Firearms dots:3
/skill_set character:Raven skill:Stealth dots:4
/skill_set character:Raven skill:Investigation dots:2

docs.title: Option 3: Bulk Setting
docs.text:
Set multiple skills at once:

/skill_bulk character:Raven skill1:Firearms dots1:3 skill2:Stealth dots2:4 skill3:Investigation dots3:2

docs.title: Adding Specialties
docs.text:
Give your character specialties for bonus dice in specific situations:

/specialty character:Raven skill:Firearms specialty:Pistols
/specialty character:Raven skill:Stealth specialty:Urban

When you roll with a specialty, you get +1 die if the situation matches!

docs.title: Choose Your Creed
docs.text:
Every Hunter has a Creed that defines their philosophy:

/creed character:Raven creed:Avenger

Available Creeds: - Avenger - Defender - Innocent - Judge - Martyr - Redeemer - Visionary

docs.title: Set Your Drives
docs.text:
Define what motivates your Hunter:

# Long-term goal
/ambition character:Raven ambition:"Rid the city of vampires"

# Short-term goal  
/desire character:Raven desire:"Find the creature that killed my sister"

# Hunting motivation
/drive character:Raven drive:"Protect the innocent from supernatural predators"

docs.title: Set Edge
docs.text:
Edge represents supernatural insight and power:

/edge character:Raven edge:2

Edge Rating

Most starting Hunters have Edge 1-3. Edge adds dice to rolls and has special properties.

docs.title: Add Equipment
docs.text:
Track your Hunter's gear:

/equipment character:Raven equipment:"Glock 17, tactical vest, flashlight, silver knife, rosary"

docs.title: Add Notes
docs.text:
Keep notes about your character:

/notes character:Raven notes:"Former detective. Sister killed by vampire. Joined the hunt 6 months ago. Has a cat named Midnight."

docs.title: Your Character is Ready!
docs.text: 🎉 Congratulations! You now have a complete Hunter character.

docs.title: Quick Test Roll
docs.text:
Try rolling some dice with your new character:

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Firearms difficulty:2

Herald will: 1. Look up Raven's Dexterity and Firearms ratings 2. Roll the combined pool 3. Check for successes against difficulty 2 4. Display clean, readable results

docs.title: Example Character: Raven
docs.text:
Here's a complete character creation sequence:

# Create with attributes
/create name:Raven strength:2 dexterity:4 stamina:3 charisma:3 manipulation:2 composure:3 intelligence:3 wits:3 resolve:2

# Apply skill template
/skill_template character:Raven template:Balanced

# Add key skills manually
/skill_set character:Raven skill:Firearms dots:4
/skill_set character:Raven skill:Stealth dots:3
/skill_set character:Raven skill:Investigation dots:3

# Add specialties
/specialty character:Raven skill:Firearms specialty:Pistols
/specialty character:Raven skill:Stealth specialty:Urban

# Set Creed and drives
/creed character:Raven creed:Avenger
/ambition character:Raven ambition:"Rid the city of vampires"
/desire character:Raven desire:"Find the creature that killed my sister"
/drive character:Raven drive:"Protect the innocent"

# Set Edge
/edge character:Raven edge:2

# Add equipment
/equipment character:Raven equipment:"Glock 17, tactical vest, flashlight, silver knife"

# Add notes
/notes character:Raven notes:"Former detective turned Hunter after her sister was killed by a vampire."

# View the completed sheet
/sheet character:Raven

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that you have a character:

  • Learn to Roll Dice - Master H5E mechanics
  • Character Management - Organize multiple characters
  • Hunter Mechanics - Edge, Desperation, and more
  • Experience System - Improve your Hunter over time

Need Help?

  • Type /help topic:Character Creation in Discord
  • Check the FAQ
  • Join our Support Server

docs.title: Hunter Mechanics
docs.text: This page is under construction. Check back soon!

docs.title: Quick Start Guide
docs.text: Get up and running with Herald in under 5 minutes!

docs.title: 1. Add Herald to Your Server
docs.text:
Click the link below to invite Herald to your Discord server:

Invite Herald (Update this link with your actual bot invite URL)

Required Permissions

Herald needs these permissions to work properly:

  • Send Messages - To respond to commands
  • Embed Links - To display character sheets and dice results
  • Use External Emoji - To show dice results (optional but recommended)
  • Use Slash Commands - Required for all commands

docs.title: 2. Create Your First Character
docs.text:
Once Herald is in your server, create a character:

/create name:Raven

That's it! Herald creates a character with default attributes (all set to 1). You can customize it later.

Character Created!

Herald will confirm your character was created and show you the character sheet.

docs.title: 3. View Your Character Sheet
docs.text:
Check out your character anytime:

/sheet character:Raven

Herald displays: - All attributes (Physical, Social, Mental) - Derived stats (Health, Willpower) - Skills and specialties - Hunter-specific traits (Edge, Desperation, Creed, etc.) - Equipment and notes

docs.title: Basic Manual Roll
docs.text:
Roll dice manually without a character:

/roll attribute:3 skill:2 difficulty:2

This rolls 5 dice (3 + 2) and checks if you meet difficulty 2.

docs.title: Character-Based Roll
docs.text:
Roll using your character's stats:

/roll_char character:Raven attribute:Dexterity skill:Stealth difficulty:2

Herald automatically looks up Raven's Dexterity and Stealth ratings and rolls them!

docs.title: Add Attributes
docs.text:
Set your character's attributes:

/create name:Raven strength:2 dexterity:4 stamina:3 charisma:3 manipulation:2 composure:3 intelligence:3 wits:3 resolve:2

Or update them later with character progression commands.

docs.title: Add Skills
docs.text:
Set individual skills:

/skill_set character:Raven skill:Firearms dots:3
/skill_set character:Raven skill:Stealth dots:4

Or use a skill template for quick setup:

/skill_template character:Raven template:Balanced

docs.title: Add Specialties
docs.text:
Give your character specialties for bonus dice:

/specialty character:Raven skill:Firearms specialty:Pistols

docs.title: Set Your Creed
docs.text:
Choose your Hunter's Creed:

/creed character:Raven creed:Avenger

docs.title: Track Edge
docs.text:
Set your Edge rating (adds dice to rolls):

/edge character:Raven edge:3

docs.title: Track Desperation
docs.text:
When things get dangerous, track Desperation:

/desperation character:Raven desperation:2

docs.title: 🎉 You're Ready to Hunt!
docs.text: That's all you need to get started! Herald handles the rest.

docs.title: What's Next?
docs.text:
• Character Creation Guide - Detailed character building
  • Dice Rolling Guide - Master H5E dice mechanics
  • Command Reference - All available commands
  • Hunter Mechanics - Edge, Desperation, and more

docs.title: Quick Reference Card
docs.text:
Action Command Create character /create name:"Name" View sheet /sheet character:Name Roll with character /roll_char character:Name attribute:Attr skill:Skill Manual roll /roll attribute:# skill:# Set skill /skill_set character:Name skill:Skill dots:# Add specialty /specialty character:Name skill:Skill specialty:Name Set Edge /edge character:Name edge:# Get help /help

Need More Help?

  • Type /help in Discord for in-bot assistance
  • Visit our FAQ
  • Join the Support Discord

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text: Common questions about Herald and how to use it.

docs.title: What is Herald?
docs.text: Herald is a Discord bot specifically designed for Hunter: The Reckoning 5th Edition. It handles character management, dice rolling with H5E mechanics, Edge and Desperation tracking, and character progression.

docs.title: Is Herald free?
docs.text: Yes! Herald is completely free to use. There are no premium tiers or paid features.

docs.title: How do I add Herald to my server?
docs.text: See the Adding Herald guide for step-by-step instructions.

docs.title: What permissions does Herald need?
docs.text: Herald needs: - Send Messages (required) - Embed Links (required) - Use External Emoji (recommended for dice displays)

docs.title: Can I use Herald for other World of Darkness games?
docs.text: Herald is built specifically for Hunter: The Reckoning 5E. While some features might work for other games, it's optimized for H5E mechanics (Edge, Desperation, etc.).

docs.title: How many characters can I have?
docs.text: There's no hard limit! You can create as many characters as you need across different servers.

docs.title: Are my characters private?
docs.text: Character data is stored per-server. Your character "Raven" on Server A is separate from "Raven" on Server B.

docs.title: Can I transfer characters between servers?
docs.text: Not currently. Each server maintains its own character database.

docs.title: I deleted my character by accident! Can I get it back?
docs.text: Unfortunately, character deletion is permanent and cannot be undone. Always use the confirmation button carefully!

docs.title: Can I rename my character?
docs.text: Yes! Use /rename old_name:Raven new_name:Corvus to rename a character.

docs.title: Why are my dice showing as text instead of emojis?
docs.text:
Herald uses custom emojis for dice. If you see text, either: 1. Herald doesn't have the Use External Emoji permission 2. Your Discord settings have website previews disabled

Herald automatically falls back to text mode if emojis aren't available.

docs.title: What do the different dice symbols mean?
docs.text:
• 🎲 with red numbers = Desperation dice
  • 🎲 with gray numbers = Regular dice
  • 10 = Critical (2 successes)
  • 6-9 = Success (1 success)
  • 1-5 = Failure

docs.title: What's a "Messy Critical"?
docs.text: A messy critical happens when Desperation dice roll 10s. You get the successes, but something goes wrong. Your Storyteller will describe the complication.

docs.title: How does difficulty work?
docs.text: Difficulty is the number of successes you need. It's not the target number for dice - Herald uses the H5E system where 6+ is always a success.

docs.title: Can I roll without a character?
docs.text: Yes! Use /roll attribute:3 skill:2 to roll manually without character stats.

docs.title: What is Edge?
docs.text: Edge represents a Hunter's supernatural insight and power. It adds dice to your rolls and has special properties in H5E.

docs.title: When should I use Desperation dice?
docs.text: Desperation dice are typically used when: - You're in grave danger - You're desperate to succeed - Your Storyteller calls for it - You have a Desperation rating

docs.title: Do Desperation dice replace regular dice?
docs.text: No! Desperation dice are added to your pool. They're shown separately because they can cause messy criticals.

docs.title: How do I track Desperation changes?
docs.text: Use /desperation character:Raven desperation:3 to update your Desperation rating.

docs.title: How do skill templates work?
docs.text: Skill templates apply H5E-standard skill distributions: - Balanced - Well-rounded (7/5/3/1) - Specialist - Focused expert (9/5/1/0) - Jack-of-all-trades - Broad competence (5/5/5/0)

docs.title: Can I mix templates and manual skills?
docs.text: Yes! Apply a template for a quick start, then use /skill_set to customize specific skills.

docs.title: How does XP spending work?
docs.text: Use /spend_xp to improve attributes or skills. Herald automatically: - Deducts the XP cost - Updates the trait - Recalculates derived stats - Logs the transaction

docs.title: What are specialties?
docs.text:
Specialties are areas of expertise within skills. When your specialty applies, you get +1 die to rolls.

Example: Firearms (Pistols) gives +1 die when using pistols.

docs.title: Why aren't slash commands showing up?
docs.text: If commands aren't appearing: 1. Wait a few minutes - Discord can be slow to sync 2. Check Herald has Use Application Commands permission 3. Ensure the @everyone role can use slash commands 4. Try restarting Discord 5. Type the command anyway - it might work!

docs.title: Can I use text commands instead of slash commands?
docs.text: No, Herald only uses Discord's slash command system. This provides better autocomplete and clearer parameter names.

docs.title: Do I need to type full character names?
docs.text: No! Herald supports autocomplete. Start typing and it will suggest your characters.

docs.title: Herald isn't responding to commands
docs.text: Check: 1. Herald is online (green status) 2. Herald has permissions in the channel 3. You're using slash commands (start with /) 4. Commands are synced (may take a few minutes after adding Herald)

docs.title: I'm getting "Character not found" errors
docs.text:
Make sure: 1. Character name is spelled correctly (case doesn't matter) 2. Character was created on this server 3. You own the character (you created it)

Use /characters to list all your characters on the server.

docs.title: Dice rolls look wrong
docs.text:
Herald follows H5E rules exactly: - 6-9 = 1 success - 10 = 2 successes (critical) - 1-5 = no success

If you think there's a bug, report it in our Support Server.

docs.title: My character sheet is cut off
docs.text: Discord has limits on embed size. If your character has extensive equipment or notes, consider shortening them or splitting across multiple fields.

docs.title: Can I restrict who can use Herald?
docs.text: Yes! Use Discord's slash command permissions in Server Settings → Integrations → Herald to control who can use commands.

docs.title: Can I limit Herald to specific channels?
docs.text: Yes! You can: 1. Remove Herald's permissions from unwanted channels 2. Use Discord's command channel overrides 3. Set up a dedicated #herald-rolls channel

docs.title: Does Herald log anything?
docs.text:
Herald logs: - XP transactions (viewable with /xp_log) - Character creation/deletion (local to each server)

Herald does NOT log: - Roll results - Private character data - User messages

docs.title: Where can I report bugs?
docs.text:
• Join our Support Discord
  • Post in the #bug-reports channel
  • Include screenshots if possible

docs.title: Can I suggest features?
docs.text: Absolutely! Feature requests are welcome: - Post in the #suggestions channel on our Discord - Describe what you want and why it would help

docs.title: Is there a roadmap?
docs.text: Check our Support Discord for announcements about upcoming features.

docs.title: How can I contribute?
docs.text: Herald is open source! Visit our GitHub repository to: - Report issues - Submit pull requests - Improve documentation - Help other users

docs.title: Still Have Questions?
docs.text:
• Join our Support Server - Get help from the community
  • Check Common Issues - Solutions to frequent problems
  • Read the Guides - Detailed tutorials

Can't find your answer? Ask in our Discord server - we're here to help!
